description_long: This apartment building features a Mediterranean-inspired design with a prominent terracotta-tiled roof and stucco exterior walls in a warm beige tone. The front facade is symmetrical, with a central entrance flanked by two projecting sections, each with its own small set of stairs and planters. Large windows are regularly spaced along the sides and rear, providing ample natural light to the interior. The building includes a garage with a white door, a visible chimney, and several balconies at the rear, enhancing outdoor living space. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with the rear section extending further back and featuring more glass and open balcony railings. The overall appearance is both elegant and functional, suitable for upscale urban or suburban neighborhoods.
description: A Mediterranean-style two-story apartment building with a terracotta-tiled roof, stucco walls, and multiple balconies. The facade features a central entrance, garage, and regularly spaced windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, two planters, a garage door, and symmetrical facade details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the building's L-shaped layout, tiled roof, and window arrangement along the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying large glass windows, multiple balconies, and the extended back section of the building.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain stucco wall, roofline, and a glimpse of the front and rear sections.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows, with regular spacing along the front, sides, and rear. Larger glass panels and grouped windows are present at the back, especially near the balconies.
